channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Crabtree-Ireland: Leading SAG-AFTRA in a New Era of AI
item.description: Duncan Crabtree-Ireland on AI, actors' rights, and leading SAG-AFTRA into the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Snow, Fast Company

item.title: YouTube is taking over TV
item.description: YouTube was launched in February 2005 and soon adopted a simple motto: “Broadcast Yourself.” While we initially watched on PCs, and then smartphones, it seems that these days an increasing number of us are engaging with YouTube content on our televisions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Peter Thiel Discusses Politics and Trump Administration Role
item.description: Peter Thiel, tech billionaire and PayPal co-founder, shares his views on politics and decision not to have a full-time role in President-elect Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New IOCONTROL Malware Targets Critical Infrastructure Organizations
item.description: Iranian state-sponsored threat actors target IoT devices and OT/SCADA systems in critical infrastructure organizations with modular malware called IOCONTROL.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Discover’s hybrid cloud journey pays off
item.description: Discover Financial Services has moved aggressively to the cloud in 2024 with a migration strategy focused on retaining hybrid flexibility and making the most of cloud elasticity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world's rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: Scientists suspect the first complete specimen ever recorded of the world’s rarest whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: If you invested in Apple’s IPO 44 years ago, here’s what your shares would be worth today
item.description: On December 12, 1980, a few hours after the opening bell rang on Wall Street, shares of Apple Computer officially began trading. The numbers, by today’s standards, weren’t that impressive.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s pick of David Sacks as AI and crypto czar shows how cozy his White House will be with Silicon Valley
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s approach to crypto is becoming clearer with the appointment of entrepreneur David Sacks as Trump’s “White House A.I. & Crypto Czar”—signaling a shift in how the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Trump offers support for dockworkers union by saying ports shouldn’t install more automated systems
item.description: The International Longshoremen’s Association has until Jan. 15 to negotiate a new contract with the U.S. Maritime Alliance, which represents ports and shipping companies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash is requiring its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Smart cities in action: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV project sets a new standard
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 4 key AI risks to address when contracting services or products
item.description: With the rapid rise of AI, especially GenAI, clients are evaluating risks from partner or vendor use of AI. CIOs and organizations are advised to consider how these risks may impact their operations and security and create contractual terms to address them.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok's future in jeopardy as US ban looms
item.description: TikTok’s future in the U.S. is in serious jeopardy after a federal appeals court rejected its push to overturn a law that could ban the app next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google’s AI coding entry with Jules signals tougher competition in coding tools
item.description: Google has made a significant entry into the competitive AI coding tools market with Jules, an experimental coding assistant aimed at enhancing developer workflows and challenging established tools like GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok’s Canada unit wants court review of shut-down order
item.description: TikTok’s Canadian unit said it has filed an emergency motion with Canada’s Federal Court seeking a judicial review of an order that the company shut down its operations in the country on national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Pentagon announces new AI office as it looks to deploy autonomous weapons
item.description: The Defense Department on Wednesday announced a new office focused on accelerating and adopting artificial intelligence (AI) technology for the military as it aims to deploy autonomous weapons in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: California defense contractors optimistic Trump administration could create jobs
item.description: The Reagan National Defense Survey done just after the November election found that 79% of those surveyed want the U.S. to spend more on national defense.
item.dc:creator: Erika I. Ritchie, The Mercury News

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TSA's Shift towards 5G Technology: Enhancing Connectivity and Security for Transportation Systems
item.description: TSA explores 5G technology implementation, improving connectivity and security for transportation systems, with a focus on remote locations and disaster recovery.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows iPhone users how to easily delete multiple emails and manage their inboxes to reduce digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
